If you’ve got **3–5 years of hands‑on iOS dev experience**, are passionate about building clean, accessible UIs with SwiftUI and Combine, and love turning design mock‑ups into smooth user flows, we’d love to chat.

You’ll collaborate with our product and design teams to iterate quickly, implement new features, and keep our apps snappy across all iPhone/iPad generations. Good to know: we’re looking for someone who’s comfortable navigating newer Xcode releases, unit‑testing their work, and contributing to CI/CD pipelines. Remote‑first culture, but we’re open to occasional HQ visits (San Francisco, Seattle, or Toronto). If you’re excited to grow with a fast‑moving team and bring your passion for great mobile experiences, drop us a line!

- **Position:** Senior Red‑Team Lead – Cloud IAM  
- **Experience:** 6+ years in red‑team/ethical hacking, 2+ years architecting & testing IAM solutions (Azure, AWS, Google Cloud).  
- **Certifications:** OSCP, CEH, CISSP (or equivalent).  
- **Technical Stack:**  
  - Proficiency in SAML, OIDC, OAuth, and fine‑grained token controls.  
  - Hands‑on with Metasploit, Burp Suite, Pass-the-Hash/Token, Azure Sentinel, CloudTrail, and SIEM analytics.  
- **Key Responsibilities:**  
  - Lead a 4‑person penetration testing team focused on cloud IAM exploits.

- **Delivery Model:** US‑based, hybrid (NYC) or fully remote; immediate start, 12‑month contract with full‑time commitment.

anchor:
🚀 Hey folks! We’re looking for a **Junior SRE (Observability)** to jump into our fast‑moving data‑pipeline team in Seattle (or fully remote). Think of a place where you can build dashboards, monitor data‑flow health and keep our Kafka + Flink streams humming 24/7. 

**What we want:**
- 1‑3 yrs of ops or system‑engineering experience (no K8s required, just good on basic Linux, networking & scripting).  
- Hands‑on with Prometheus, Grafana and an **ELK stack** (Elastic Search + Kibana) for real‑time logging.  
- Comfortable writing **Python/Bash** automation for alert tuning and log parsing.  
- Experience with **Terraform** or other IaC is a plus—ideally you love the Dev‑Sec‑Ops mindset.  
- You’re eager to work with our data scientists and help keep our GCP infrastructure robust (Pub/Sub, CloudSQL, BigQuery).  

**Why join us?**  
- We’re growing faster than ever—your work will directly impact millions of users.  
- “Future‑first” mentality: we give you the chance to propose infra improvements, experiment with new observability tools, and publish paper‑quality automation.  
- Flexible schedule—remote friendly, but a week‑a‑month on‑site visit keeps the team tight.  

**Ready?** Drop me a quick DM or ping me with your updated CV. Let’s chat about the next level you want to build in SRE!

Deployed GCP infrastructure with Terraform, managing Pub/Sub, CloudSQL, and BigQuery. Collaborated with data scientists to maintain 99.9% uptime.

anchor:
Hey team 👋 we’re hunting for a **SRE Automation / DevOps Engineer** to join our obs & reli platform squad.  
What we need:  

- Mid‑level (2‑5 yr exp) – not the senior SRE post we already have.  
- Primarily focus on building & maintaining telemetry pipelines **in GCP** (Stackdriver/Cloud‑Ops, Cloud Logging, Cloud Trace).  
- Must own the IaC: Terraform, Pulumi, or even GitHub Actions workflows.  
- Scripting in Go, Python **or** TypeScript – pick the stack that’s fresh.  
- Can pull heavy metrics/trace loads (Mil metrics/s, 50k traces/s) and keep them under control.  
- Must design & tweak alert rules in Prometheus / OpenTelemetry, run the real‑time alerting matrix, and keep PagerDuty/Pulse on‑call sane.  
- Hands‑on with Grafana dashboards (no UI‑less mgmt mode) – build & document look‑ups for ops.  
- Good at CI/CD and GitOps – ArgoCD or Spinnaker bro.  
- 1‑2 certifications fine (e.g., TA‑CICD, AWS CDK, or GCP’s Data‑Op).  
- No need for US‑only; we are a fully remote squad (US West, East, or other).  
- Offer: $110‑130 k + bonus + equity; 3‑day remote per week.  
- Start ASAP or within 4‑6 wk.  

Drop a DM if you or someone you know fits 📩.

anchor:
- **Role**: Senior Mobile Security Engineer (iOS & Android)  
- **Seniority**: 5+ years of mobile security experience, with a proven track record of protecting production apps from OWASP ASVS level 1‑5 vulnerabilities  
- **Platforms**: Deep expertise in both modern iOS (Swift, Objective‑C) and Android (Kotlin/Java), plus experience with iOS App Transport Security, Android Keystore, NVMe, and Secure Element APIs  
- **Tooling**: Practical knowledge of static & dynamic analysis (e.g., OWASP ZAP, Mobile Security Framework), reverse‑engineering tools (Frida, Hopper), and secure coding libraries (ProGuard, R8, SwiftLint, AppCode security plugins)  
- **Responsibilities**:  
  - Lead a comprehensive mobile security audit and threat‑modeling exercise for new releases  
  - Define and maintain secure coding guidelines, best‑practice playbooks, and penetration‑test procedures  
  - Mentor junior security engineers and publish internal training sessions/workshops  
  - Collaborate with product, DevOps, and QA teams to integrate security into CI/CD pipelines (Spectra, Fastlane, Bitrise, GitHub Actions)  
- **Experience**:  
  - 3+ years conducting mobile security reviews for high‑traffic consumer apps or fintech platforms  
  - Familiarity with iOS Data Protection classes, jailbreak detection, Android runtime permissions, and Android NDK security  
  - Experience with secure storage patterns (Keychain, CredentialManager, Tink, ChaCha20-Poly1305) and encryption‑at‑rest protocols  
- **Additional Requirements**:  
  - Open source contributors on mobile security tools preferred  
  - Strong writing skills for security documentation, audit reports, and compliance certifications (SOC 2, PCI‑DSS for mobile)  
  - Ability to travel up to 25 % for on‑site security workshops and client engagements  
- **Compensation & Benefits**: Competitive salary plus bonus and equity; full coverage health, vision, dental; 4‑week PTO; relocation stipend if applicable  
- **Location & Work Model**: Remote‑first, but some in‑office presence (NYC/Columbus) accepted depending on role; no C2C.  
- **Application deadline**: Start date 4‑6 weeks after interview, priority for candidates ready to transition immediately.

anchor:
**Position:** Mid‑Level Backend Engineer (Python/Django, Go)  

**Company Overview:**  
Midjourney Technologies is accelerating the development of enterprise‑grade data platforms for financial services. We build resilient, high‑throughput APIs that serve millions of daily transactions across cloud environments.  

**Role Summary:**  
We seek a highly driven Back‑End Engineer to join our core platform team. The successful candidate will design, implement, and maintain scalable micro‑services in Python/Django, while extending performance‑critical subsystems in Go. You will collaborate closely with DevOps, Data Science, and Product teams to deliver secure, efficient, and maintainable code at a rapid velocity.  

**Core Responsibilities**  
- Craft clean, test‑driven micro‑service solutions in Python/Django, integrating with PostgreSQL, Redis, and Kafka.  
- Optimize hot‑paths in Go for multi‑threaded data ingestion and real‑time analytics.  
- Own the deployment pipeline (CI/CD) on AWS using ECS, Lambda, and CodePipeline, and champion infrastructure‑as‑code with Terraform.  
- Conduct code reviews, enforce architectural guidelines, and mentor junior engineers on best practices.  
- Participate in capacity planning, cost‑optimization projects, and fail‑over testing for high‑availability services.  

**Required Qualifications**  
- 4–6 years of professional software development experience in a backend capacity.  
- Proficiency in Python (≥3.8) with Django, coupled with solid experience in Go (≥1.16).  
- Demonstrated exposure to containerization (Docker), orchestration (Kubernetes), and cloud‑native services on AWS or comparable cloud platforms.  
- Proven track record of designing and scaling RESTful/GraphQL APIs, and conducting performance tuning (profiling, benchmarking).  
- Experience writing and maintaining unit, integration, and performance tests (PyTest, GoTest).  
- Familiarity with CI/CD tooling (GitHub Actions, Jenkins, GitLab CI) and IaC (Terraform, Pulumi).  

**Preferred Traits**  
- Exposure to asynchronous programming frameworks (Celery, FastAPI, or equivalent).  
- Prior work on real‑time analytics pipelines or streaming data solutions (Kafka, Pulsar).  
- Ability to communicate complex technical solutions clearly to non‑technical stakeholders.  

**Compensation & Benefits**  
- Competitive salary range commensurate with experience.  
- Year‑long signing bonus potential and relocation package.  
- Health, dental, vision, 401(k) with company match, and flexible paid‑time‑off.  

**Work Model**  
Full‑time, hybrid schedule: 3 days onsite at our New York City office, 2 days remote. We also offer fully remote positions for exceptional candidates.  

**How to Apply**  
Please submit your résumé and a concise cover letter (200 words) detailing a recent backend project where you integrated a new technology stack, and quantify the business impact achieved.  

*We are an equal‑opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and encourage candidates from all backgrounds to apply.*

anchor:
Seeking a **Mid‑Level Frontend Engineer** (3–5 years of professional experience) with a proven track record in React, Next.js, and Tailwind CSS. The candidate will collaborate closely with product, design, and DevOps teams to deliver pixel‑perfect, high‑performance web interfaces. Responsibilities include:

* Building reusable, accessible React components and integrating Tailwind utilities in a modular architecture  
* Contributing to server‑side rendering (SSR) and incremental static regeneration (ISR) strategy in a Next.js + JAMstack environment  
* Implementing TypeScript strongly‑typed components and working with localGraphQL or RESTful APIs, ensuring robust type safety and error handling  
* Op‑timizing build pipelines, automating quality gates with ESLint, Prettier, and Husky, and configuring CI/CD workflows (GitHub Actions / GitLab CI) for seamless deployments  
* Writing unit, integration, and visual regression tests using Jest, React Testing Library, and Playwright, and maintaining a high code‑coverage benchmark  
* Championing web‑performance best practices (code‑splitting, dynamic imports, image optimization) and accessibility (WCAG 2.1 AA) compliance across the product line  

**Required Qualifications**

* 3–5 years of frontend production experience in a professional setting  
* Strong command of modern JavaScript (ES6+), TypeScript, and functional component patterns (hooks, context)  
* Experience implementing Tailwind CSS with custom design tokens and utility‑first methodologies  
* Familiarity with headless CMS integrations (Strapi, Sanity, Contentful) and GraphQL client libraries (Apollo, urql)  
* Demonstrated proficiency in performance metrics (Lighthouse, web vitals) and techniques to reduce bundle size  
* Excellent code‑reviewing habits, cross‑functional communication skills, and an aptitude for mentoring junior team members  

**Preferred Extras**

* Knowledge of state‑management libraries (Recoil, Zustand, or Redux Toolkit)

Seeking a **Mid‑Level Frontend Engineer** (3–5 years of professional experience) with a proven track record in React, Next.js, and Tailwind CSS. The candidate will collaborate closely with product, design, and DevOps teams to deliver pixel‑perfect, high‑performance web interfaces. Responsibilities include:

* Building reusable, accessible React components and integrating Tailwind utilities in a modular architecture  
* Contributing to server‑side rendering (SSR) and incremental static regeneration (ISR) strategy in a Next.js + JAMstack environment  
* Implementing TypeScript strongly‑typed components and working with localGraphQL or RESTful APIs, ensuring robust type safety and error handling  
* Op‑timizing build pipelines, automating quality gates with ESLint, Prettier, and Husky, and configuring CI/CD workflows (GitHub Actions / GitLab CI) for seamless deployments  
* Writing unit, integration, and visual regression tests using Jest, React Testing Library, and Playwright, and maintaining a high code‑coverage benchmark  
* Championing web‑performance best practices (code‑splitting, dynamic imports, image optimization) and accessibility (WCAG 2.1 AA) compliance across the product line  

**Required Qualifications**

* 3–5 years of frontend production experience in a professional setting  
* Strong command of modern JavaScript (ES6+), TypeScript, and functional component patterns (hooks, context)  
* Experience implementing Tailwind CSS with custom design tokens and utility‑first methodologies  
* Familiarity with headless CMS integrations (Strapi, Sanity, Contentful) and GraphQL client libraries (Apollo, urql)  
* Demonstrated proficiency in performance metrics (Lighthouse, web vitals) and techniques to reduce bundle size  
* Excellent code‑reviewing habits, cross‑functional communication skills, and an aptitude for mentoring junior team members

* Knowledge of state‑management libraries (Recoil, Zustand, or Redux Toolkit)

* Familiarity with Docker containers and Kubernetes deployment strategies

Looking for a **Mid‑Level Frontend Engineer** (React 18, Next.js 12 – classic _app_ folder, not the new App Router) with **2‑3 yrs of hands‑on web dev** experience. The role will focus on **performance‑first** builds: ⚡️PWA (service workers, manifest), 🎨 CSS‑Modules + Styled‑Components, and lightweight _Tailwind JIT_ (v3) usage for rapid prototyping.  

**Must‑haves:**  
- Solid cash‑in‑hand on **Webpack 5**/Rollup config tweaking, code‑splitting, and tree‑shaking.

- Familiarity with **GraphQL (Apollo Client 3)** and **REST (Axios 0.22)**, but no strong TypeScript requirement – we’ll use JavaScript for now.  
- Practical experience with **Accessibility (WCAG 2.1)**, **a11y testing** via axe or Totlok.

**Bonus:**  
- Knowing **React‑Native Web** for cross‑platform hooks.  
- Prior work on **clean‑room design systems** or participated in dev‑ops / A/B testing.

Mid‑Level iOS Developer (Swift 5.8 / SwiftUI) – 3 – 5 yrs of hands‑on experience building high‑quality, production‑grade mobile applications for subscription‑based SaaS platforms on iOS 17+.  

**Key Technical Requirements**  
- Mastery of Swift 5.8, SwiftUI, UIKit, and Combine – with deep knowledge of async/await, Swift’s concurrency model, and the new Swift Data API.  
- Strong background in native iOS architectural patterns (MVVM/Redux‑style), Clean Architecture, and dependency injection (Swinject/Resolver).  
- Proficient with data persistence using Core Data, Realm, or SQLite, and cloud‑centric services (CloudKit, AWS Amplify, Firebase).  
- Experienced in building reusable UI component libraries (Swift Packages) that expose concise, Swift‑UI‑friendly APIs.  
- Familiar with Xcode Cloud, Fastlane, and other CI/CD pipelines for building, testing, and distributing iOS apps.  

**Testing & Quality Expectations**  
- Contribute to a comprehensive automated testing suite using XCUITest, Quick/Nimble, and custom UI testing frameworks.  
- Implement unit, integration, and UI regression tests; analyze and report test coverage metrics.  

**Collaboration & Process**  
- Write clear, maintainable code with comprehensive documentation; lead small code‑review discussions—no high‑level architecture responsibilities required.  
- Contribute to an internal VCS guidelines handbook; maintain knowledge base articles on new Swift features and best practices.  

**Other Qualifications**  
- At least 3 – 5 years of full‑cycle iOS development experience; GitHub presence with notable open‑source Swift projects or educational contributions.  
- Comfortable working remotely (US time zones) with occasional on‑site meetings in a focal city.  

**Contract Details**  
- Remote, contract‑to‑hire, 100 % flexibility on location; salary between $90 k – 115 k (USD) based on experience.  
- Start date: within 8–12 weeks of acceptance.

- **Seniority**: 3‑5 years of production app development (not senior or lead)  
- **Tech stack**: React Native 0.75+, TypeScript, Expo SDK 51, Redux Toolkit, React Navigation, React Native Testing Library, Jest, Detox for E2E testing; on the native side: Swift 5.8, UIKit/SwiftUI, Xcode 15.1, CocoaPods 1.13, Swift Package Manager, and Android Java/Kotlin 1.6, Android Studio 2023, Jetpack Compose 1.4, Gradle 8.0, Play‑Services SDK.  
- **Responsibilities**: Build reusable components, bridge native modules where needed, implement push‑notification workflows (Firebase Cloud Messaging, APNs), integrate analytics (Amplitude, Mixpanel), and participate in daily stand‑ups, code reviews, and sprint planning.  
- **CI/CD**: Experience configuring Fastlane, Bitrise, GitHub Actions (iOS build & signing, Android APK / AAB generation) and managing app‑store deployments.  
- **Other**: Comfortable working in a remote, cross‑time‑zone team (PST/EST overlap), English speaking, and a plus if you’ve earned an Apple or Google mobile developer certification.  

We’re looking for someone who can start within 4–6 weeks on a full‑time, hybrid or on‑site role (depending on location).

anchor:
**We’re seeking a seasoned DevOps Engineer to architect and maintain the infrastructure that powers our high‑traffic MERN‑GraphQL SaaS platform.**  
If you’re a mid‑to‑senior engineer (3–5 yrs senior‑level experience) who thrives on automating, scaling, and securing cloud‑native workloads, we want you on our team.

**Core responsibilities**  
- Design, deploy, and operate production‑grade Kubernetes clusters (v1.28 or newer) on AWS using **Amazon EKS** and **AWS Fargate**.  
- Build and maintain CI‑CD pipelines with **GitHub Actions** and **Argo CD**, ensuring zero‑downtime deployments for our **Node.js 20 / TypeScript 5** back‑end (NestJS v9) and **React 18** front‑end.  
- Implement infrastructure as code using **Terraform 1.4** and **AWS CDK v2.30**, codifying IAM policies, VPCs, ALBs, RDS (PostgreSQL 15), and ElasticCache (Redis 7).  
- Define and enforce container image best‑practices: Docker Compose 3.7, multi‑stage builds, signing with JFrog Xray, and secure registry hosting.  
- Manage observability stacks (Prometheus v2.53, Grafana 9, Loki 2.8) and log aggregation through **AWS CloudWatch** and **Elastic Stack (ELK)**.  
- Conduct capacity planning, auto‑scaling, and cost‑optimization reviews (Spot instances, Savings Plans).  
- Lead disaster‑recovery drills and develop SLOs/SLO dashboards for uptime>99.95 %.  
- Mentor junior DevOps & SRE staff, conduct pair‑programming and architecture workshops.

**Required skills**  
- Proven experience deploying large‑scale Node.js/TypeScript services on Kubernetes with Helm v3.9.  
- Infrastructure‑as‑Code expertise: Terraform patterning, AWS CDK, CloudFormation.  
- Deep knowledge of container networking, **ServiceMesh (Istio 1.18+)**, and ingress controllers (NGINX, ALB).  
- Automation with **GitHub Actions**, **ArgoCD**, **Jenkins X**, or similar pipelines.  
- Experience with **Prometheus/Alertmanager**, **Grafana dashboards**, **ELK**, and **OpenTelemetry**.  
- Familiarity with **GraphQL** servers (Apollo v4.x, TypeGraphQL) on the back‑end, ensuring proper resource allocation for query explosion scenarios.  
- Strong analytical mindset, debugging at scale, and a passion for security: network policies, secrets management (AWS Secrets Manager, HashiCorp Vault).  
- Excellent communication skills; able to articulate infra design to product, security, and design teams.

**Preferred**  
- Container security scanning with **Trivy**, **Anchore**, or **Syft**.  
- Experience with **Serverless Framework** or **AWS Lambda** (Node 20).  
- Understanding of CI‑CD security gates: Snyk IaC scanning, binary signing.  
- Cloud‑native networking (CNI, Calico), or experience building a custom overlay network.  

**What we’re offering**  
- Fully remote, work-from‑home setup funded (high‑speed VPN, hardware stipend).  
- Competitive salary + equity, 100 % health, vision & dental, 30 days PTO, unlimited sick time.  
- Fast‑paced startup culture with room to own infra architecture from day‑one and shape our future tech roadmap.  
- 24/7/365 uptime expectations drive innovation—you're part of our mission to deliver the best SaaS at scale.  

**Apply today** if you’re ready to own the entire infrastructure—deployment, reliability, performance, and cost—while collaborating with a high‑velocity MERN–GraphQL team.  No C2C agreements accepted; we seek fully employed, remote‑capable candidates available for core PT‑time coordination.  Expected start 4 weeks after hiring decision.

work_experience.description: Implemented Kubernetes clusters on AWS EKS for microservices. Developed Helm charts, set up Docker Compose pipelines, integrated with GitHub Actions for CI. Managed logging via ELK and CloudWatch. Contributed to scaling and monitoring of Node.js back‑end and React front‑end. Authored automation scripts in Python and Bash.

Architected and maintained scalable Kubernetes clusters (v1.27) on AWS EKS and Fargate for a high‑traffic SaaS platform. Implemented CI‑CD with GitHub Actions and Argo CD, enabling zero‑downtime deployments for Node.js/TypeScript services orchestrated via Helm 3.9. Developed IaC using Terraform 1.4 and CDK v2.30, codified IAM, VPC, ALB, RDS PostgreSQL 15, and ElasticCache Redis 7. Introduced service mesh with Istio, managed network policies and secrets using AWS Secrets Manager and Vault. Built observability with Prometheus, Grafana, and AWS CloudWatch. Led cost‑optimization initiatives and performed disaster‑recovery drills.

anchor:
hey folks, we’re on the hunt for a Senior SOC Architect (Level 3+). 6+ yrs of hands‑on experience designing and running large‑scale Security Operations Centers, and you’ve already led a migration from Gen‑II to Gen‑III SIEM platforms while keeping the end‑user experience smooth.  

**Must‑have tech stack**  
- Splunk Enterprise 8.2 (or later) – full‑blown role‑based access, custom dashboards, ML/SIEM apps, and indexing strategies for 50+ TB daily logs.  
- ELK Stack 7.12 – mastery of Beats, Logstash pipelines, Kibana 7.x for threat hunting and EQL.  
- SOAR/Automation – IBM QRadar SOAR (pro & enterprise) or Cortex XSOAR (v3.4) integration and playbook authoring.  
- GCP Security – Cloud Armor, BeyondCorp, Cloud DLP, and Pub/Sub pub‑sub security monitoring.  
- IDP – Okta 7.6+ with custom SAML flows for internal applications.  

**Desired certifications**  
- CISSP (or equivalent).  
- Splunk Certified Advanced Power User.  
- Threat Intelligence Platform (TIP) Certified.  

**Other hard requirements**  
- Proven ability to choreograph SOAR playbooks using Python 3.10, Typescript, and Ansible.  
- Experience building and maintaining enterprise‑level EDR with CrowdStrike Falcon™ (8.1+) or SentinelOne (1.23+).  
- GPT‑integrated feeds for real‑time threat intel parsing.  
- At least 2 years of continuous monitoring and incident response on a Gen‑III SOC with 24/7 coverage.  

We’re looking for someone who can start Monday, June 18th, full‑time, remote (open to any U.S. state but a preference for Eastern Time).  U.S. citizenship is required (no CO‑C, no contract work).  Must be fluent in English and comfortable presenting findings to senior execs.  

Drop a resume that highlights your SIEM migration story, SOAR playbook examples, and version specifics. DM me immediately. 🚀

Architect and operate 24/7 SOC for Fortune 500 clients, managing real‑time incident response on a Gen‑III SOC. Develop and maintain SOAR playbooks with Python 3.10, Typescript, and Ansible. Implement GCP Pub/Sub security monitoring, enhance EDR scalability with CrowdStrike Falcon, and deliver executive dashboards with Splunk Enterprise and ELK Stack.

🔎 **Open Position: Mid‑Level Embedded Firmware Engineer (ARM Cortex‑M7, FreeRTOS, Python Test Automation)**  
💡 *Domain: Embedded & Systems (C/C++ & RTOS, FPGA)*  

**What we need:**  
- 3–5 years of hands‑on firmware development on ARM Cortex‑M7 platforms (e.g., NXP LPC5500, STMicro STM32H7).  
- Deep knowledge of **FreeRTOS 10.x** (task priorities, queues, semaphores, timers) and **CMSIS‑RTOS** API.  
- Proficiency in **C/C++** (C99/C++11) with *IAR Embedded Workbench 8.32* or *Keil MDK‑ARM v5.66*, including experience optimizing for memory footprint and power‑down modes.  
- Experience writing **Python 3.10+** based test harnesses (PyTest, unittest) that interface with hardware via *pySerial*, *pyUSB*, and *pyCAN*.  
- Basic FPGA/HDL experience (VHDL 2008) – able to write simple IP blocks or interface with existing IP for peripheral control.  
- Familiarity with Git (GitHub/GitLab) and CI pipelines (GitLab CI, Jenkins) for automated firmware builds and regression testing.  

**Ideal candidate profile:**  
- Comfortable in a *remote* environment, motivated to iterate quickly with a distributed engineering team.  
- No C2C; must be available for **U.S. W2 employment** (citizenship or permanent residency required).  
- Able to relocate within 30 days if offered an on‑site role (optional).  
- Background check required, no security clearance required.  

**What we offer:**  
- Competitive salary + 401(k) match, comprehensive health benefits.  
- Flexible schedule, continuous learning stipend.  

💬 DM me with a résumé that lists: project experience on Cortex‑M7/Freertos boards, links to any GitHub repos or test‑automation scripts, and the specific EWB/Keil version you used. Looking for someone to start in the next 30 days! Happy hiring!

work_experience.description: Worked on Cortex-M7 boards for IoT devices, using Keil MDK-ARM 5.66, built modular firmware with FreeRTOS 10, and created automated test harnesses with PyTest.

Developed firmware for Cortex-M7 based motor control modules, optimized memory footprint, implemented power-down modes, and integrated FreeRTOS 10 and CMSIS-RTOS APIs. Collaborated with hardware team for FPGA integration.

anchor:
Hi team,

We’re hunting for a **senior full‑stack engineer** to lead our next phase of the customer‑portal rewrite. The ideal candidate will bring 5+ years of production experience and a deep mastery of the following stack:

| Area | Tool / Version | What we expect |
|------|----------------|-----------------|
| **Front‑end** | React 18 + Redux Toolkit + React Query 5 | Build high‑performance, accessible UIs with a component‑first approach |
| **Framework** | Next.js 13 (App Router) | Server‑side rendering, incremental static regeneration, API routes |
| **Back‑end** | Node 18 + Express 4 + TypeScript 5 | Clean, typed API implementation, strong error handling |
| **Database** | MongoDB 7 + Mongoose 7 | Schema design, indexing, aggregation pipelines |
| **GraphQL** | Apollo Server 4 + GraphQL 16 | Schema stitching, auth directives, real‑time subscriptions |
| **Caching** | Redis 7 (via ioredis) | Query caching, session store |
| **Containerisation** | Docker 24 + Docker‑Compose 2 | Build and push multi‑stage images, health‑checks |
| **Orchestration** | Kubernetes 1.29 + Helm 3 | Deploy to a managed cluster (EKS/GKE), Helm charts for CI‑CD |
| **Infrastructure** | Terraform 1.6 + AWS Fargate or GKE | IaC, cost optimisation, CI pipeline |
| **Testing** | Jest 29 + Supertest + Cypress | Unit, integration, end‑to‑end coverage ≥ 85 % |
| **CI / CD** | GitHub Actions + ArgoCD | Automated linting, testing, image builds, automated roll‑outs |

### Role specifics
- **Seniority:** Lead / Senior engineer level
- **Location:** Remote from any US time zone (prefer West Coast for sea‑level sync)
- **Start date:** Must be able to join end‑of‑Q2, ideally within 2 weeks.
- **Employment type:** Full‑time, no C2C or contractors.

### Deliverables & expectations
1. Own the end‑to‑end development of new portal features – from front‑end components to GraphQL resolvers and database tuning.
2. Mentor two junior/mid engineers on the team.
3. Lead a 4‑week sprint achieving a 10 % performance lift on critical queries.
4. Deliver up‑to‑date docs on architecture, API contracts, CI/CD pipelines, and runbooks.
5. Partner with product and data teams to integrate real‑time analytics and analytics dashboards.

If you’re a seasoned MERN/GraphQL expert who thrives in a fast‑moving, mission‑critical environment, please reach out. We’re ready to onboard quickly and would love to meet you! 🚀

anchor:
- Minimum of 3–5 years of professional frontend development experience in a dynamic, product‑driven environment  
- Proven expertise in Vue 3 (Composition API, Teleport, Suspense) coupled with modern build tooling (Vite, Vue‑CLI) and TypeScript integration  
- Deep proficiency in styling ecosystems such as Tailwind CSS, CSS Modules, or scoped CSS, with a demonstrable portfolio of responsive, high‑fidelity UIs  
- Extensive knowledge of progressive web app (PWA) principles, service workers, and offline data synchronization to deliver robust, resilient user experiences  
- Track record executing end‑to‑end performance optimizations: tree‑shaking, lazy loading, critical‑path rendering, and Web Vitals monitoring (LCP, FID, CLS)  
- Strong grasp of component‑driven architecture, state management patterns (Pinia, Vuex, or reactive composables), and reusable design systems deployed across multiple products  
- Commitment to accessibility best practices (WCAG 2.2, ARIA, keyboard navigation) and automated visual testing (Playwright, Storybook + Chromatic)  
- Proficiency with version control (Git), CI/CD pipelines (GitLab CI, GitHub Actions), and containerization (Docker) for deployment pipelines  
- Fluent in modern JavaScript (ES2022+) and a solid working knowledge of RESTful APIs, GraphQL, and websockets for real‑time data interaction  
- Ability to collaborate effectively in Agile teams (Scrum, Kanban) with experience contributing to sprint planning, retrospectives, and continuous improvement initiatives  
- Excellent communication skills in English (both written and verbal), with experience presenting technical concepts to cross‑functional stakeholders  
- Availability for remote work across the U.S. time zones (preferred overlap with East Coast business hours) or hybrid onsite presence in Seattle, WA or Boston, MA  
- Preferred: Experience scaling micro‑frontend deployments or integrating with legacy monoliths, and familiarity with JAM Stack hosting (Netlify, Vercel, or Cloudflare Pages)  
- Desired: Certifications in Vue.js ecosystem (e.g., Vue.js Academy) or front‑end performance (e.g., Web Performance Institute)  
- Expected start: Ready to transition within 4–6 weeks post‑selection, with a structured onboarding plan and mentorship pairing.

We’re expanding our data‑center footprint and need a **Platform & Infrastructure Architect** who can design, secure, and scale an end‑to‑end event pipeline on **Google Cloud Platform**.  
**Key responsibilities**

anchor:
**ML Engineer – Computer Vision & TensorFlow (Full‑Time, Remote/Europe)**  
We are recruiting a junior‑level Machine Learning Engineer with 0–2 years of industry experience to develop state‑of‑the‑art computer‑vision solutions for our next‑generation product line. The ideal candidate will possess:

- Proficiency in TensorFlow 2.8+ (Keras API), PyTorch (optional), and OpenCV 4.x for image processing, feature extraction, and augmentation pipelines (Albumentations, imgaug).
- Hands‑on experience building and training convolutional neural networks (ResNet, EfficientNet, Swin‑Transformer) for multi‑class image classification, object detection, and semantic segmentation tasks on annotated datasets (COCO, Pascal VOC, custom internal datasets).
- Comfort with GPU‑based training on NVIDIA V100/T4 platforms, leveraging distributed training techniques (MirroredStrategy, Multi‑Worker MirroredStrategy) and model quantization (TensorRT, ONNX Runtime) for inference.
- Solid data‑engineering skills: Pandas, NumPy, scikit‑learn, and experience designing ETL pipelines (Apache Airflow, Prefect) for cleaning, labeling, and versioning image data.
- Ability to containerize models with Docker and K8s, and deploy them to Amazon SageMaker, GCP AI‑Platform, or Azure ML Runtime.
- Familiarity with version control (Git), CI/CD (GitHub Actions, GitLab CI), and logging/monitoring tools (Mlflow, Prometheus, Grafana).
- Excellent written and verbal communication in English; ability to document code, write technical specifications, and collaborate across cross‑functional teams.
- Must be willing to adhere to company security and compliance processes, sign an NDA and possess a valid background check.

**Seniority** – Entry‑level/Associate (0–2 yrs)  
**Location** – Fully remote, based in Europe (EU/UK/US reporting allowed)  
**Hours** – 30–40 hrs/week, occasional weekend availability for critical deployments.  
**Certifications** – Preferred: TensorFlow Developer Certificate, Certified Kubernetes Administrator (CKA).  

This is a full‑time, permanent position with competitive compensation, benefits, and a clear career progression path toward lead ML Engineer and Technical Architect roles. Recruiters, please forward qualified candidates who meet the above criteria.

anchor: Seeking a Cloud Platform Reliability Engineer to architect, secure, and sustain highly available, cost‑efficient multi‑cloud infrastructures across AWS and Azure. Requires deep expertise in Azure AD, AKS, Azure DevOps Pipelines, and IaC via Terraform; proficiency in GitHub Actions, ArgoCD, Flux, and advanced scripting in Python, PowerShell, and Bash. Proven track record in applying Site Reliability Engineering (SRE) principles, automated monitoring with Prometheus, Grafana, and Azure Monitor, and advanced security hardening of containerized environments. Desired certifications include Microsoft Certified: Azure Solutions Architect Expert and Certified Kubernetes Administrator. This full‑time role is located on‑site in Seattle, WA, with a near‑term start date and potential for hybrid arrangements.

positive.summary: Cloud Platform Reliability Engineer with 6 years of progressive experience in architecting highly available, cost‑efficient multi‑cloud infrastructures across AWS and Azure, implementing SRE principles and advanced security hardening.

work_experience.description: Managed AWS and Azure workloads, designed CI/CD pipelines with Azure DevOps and GitHub Actions, and orchestrated Kubernetes clusters using AKS and on‑prem K8s. Implemented Terraform scripts for infrastructure provisioning and automated security scans. Monitored system health using Prometheus and Grafana dashboards.

Lead design and operation of multi‑cloud (AWS + Azure) infrastructure, implementing Azure AD, AKS, Azure DevOps Pipelines, and Terraform IaC. Automated deployment pipelines using GitHub Actions, ArgoCD, and Flux. Developed security hardening scripts in PowerShell and Python, and built comprehensive observability with Prometheus, Grafana, and Azure Monitor. Applied SRE practices to reduce MTTR from 2.3h to 25m across 12 services.

anchor:
- **Position**: Cloud Infrastructure Automation Engineer (Full‑time, Remote)  
- **Level**: Mid‑Senior (3–5 years of production experience)  
- **Primary Cloud**: AWS (core services such as EC2, S3, RDS, Lambda) with secondary exposure to Azure or GCP preferred  
- **Key Technologies**:  
  - **Infrastructure‑as‑Code**: Terraform (AWS modules) + CloudFormation basics  
  - **Container Orchestration**: Kubernetes (EKS) + Helm charts  
  - **CI/CD**: GitLab CI/CD pipelines (GitLab Runner, Dockerfile builds, image scanning)  
  - **Observability**: Prometheus + Grafana, with Loki/Tempo integration for logs and tracing  
  - **Scripting**: Python 3.8+ (automation scripts, custom Terraform providers) – proficiency in Bash/PowerShell a plus  
- **Security & Compliance**: Operational knowledge of IAM roles, KMS, encryption at rest & in transit, and automated vulnerability scanning (Trivy, Aqua, etc.)  
- **Soft Skills**:  
  - Strong communication in English (written & spoken)  
  - Ability to mentor a junior automation engineer or intern  
  - Experience in documentation (Confluence, Markdown) and knowledge transfer sessions  
- **Availability**: End‑to‑end project ownership, ready to start within 2–3 weeks, flexible to occasional travel or on‑site collaboration (no strict office‑first policy).  
- **Certifications**: AWS Certified Developer or AWS Certified DevOps Engineer are a plus but not mandatory.  
- **Why Join?** Build highly‑scaled, resilient infrastructure from the ground up, influence our cloud‑native culture, and work in an open‑source‑friendly, fast‑moving team.

anchor:
**Position:** Mid‑Level Android Engineer – Kotlin & Jetpack Compose  
**Location:** Remote (Preference for East Coast alignment)  
**Seniority Level:** Mid‑Senior (5–7 years of professional experience)  
**Contract:** Full‑time, long‑term (no immediate start requirement, we’ll discuss possible start between 8–12 weeks).  

**Role Overview**  
Our rapidly expanding consumer‑facing platform requires a seasoned Android engineer to architect, develop, and maintain richly interactive native experiences. You will be embedded in a cross‑functional team that delivers feature‑critical modules at scale, collaborating closely with product, design, and backend stakeholders to deliver high‑fidelity, low‑latency experiences for millions of users worldwide.

**Key Responsibilities**  
- Spearhead the design and implementation of complex UI flows using **Jetpack Compose**, ensuring cohesive adherence to our design system.  
- Develop reusable architectural primitives (MVVM, Clean Architecture, Hilt‑DI, Flow/Coroutines) that can be leveraged across multiple squads.  
- Drive performance optimization through profiling, static analysis, and code‑level enhancements; routinely reduce app size and runtime latency.  
- Champion best practices surrounding automated UI testing (Espresso, ComposeTestRule) and unit‑integration pipelines; integrate code coverage reports into our CI system.  
- Mentor junior engineers and contribute to the growth of our internal DevOps and testing culture.  
- Own a small set of high‑priority APIs, managing end‑to‑end lifecycle from specification to deployment and roll‑out.  

**Required Experience & Skills**  
- 5+ years of professional Android development in Kotlin (Java-only experience is not acceptable).  
- Deep proficiency with **Jetpack Compose** (minimum 2 years of production work).  
- Strong grasp of **modern Android libraries** (WorkManager, Paging3, Navigation, DataStore).  
- Demonstrated ability to design and document scalable architectural patterns.  
- Proven experience with CI/CD tooling (GitLab CI, Docker, Gradle, Fastlane).  
- Comfortable programming concurrent/parallel work with Coroutines, Flow, and RxJava.  
- Experience with code‑analysis tooling (Detekt, Ktlint, SonarQube) and distributed tracing is a plus.  

**Desirable Attributes**  
- Exposure to enterprise‑grade security and data‑privacy best practices.  
- Participation in open‑source Android libraries or community contributions (GitHub, KotlinConf).  
- Familiarity with remote‑first workflows, distributed version control, and comprehensive test automation.  

**What We Offer**  
- Competitive base salary and equity package (aligned with seniority level).  
- Unlimited PTO, fully sponsored remote‑work stipend, and employee‑benefit bundle.  
- Opportunity to own end‑to‑end design decisions in a fast‑moving, impact‑focused environment.  

If you thrive in a high‑velocity product cycle, have a passion for building flawless, native experiences, and enjoy collaborating across disciplines to bring a platform‑level vision to life, we want to hear from you. Submit your résumé, portfolio links, and a brief cover letter detailing your most ambitious Android project.

anchor:
Hey team! 🙌

Need a **mid‑level Full‑Stack Engineer** (≈3‑5 yrs experience) who can rock the **Vue/Nuxt** ecosystem plus a **Node.js 20.x / TypeScript** backend. The stack vibes should be:

- **Front‑end**: Vue 3.x, Nuxt 3, Pinia for state, TailwindCSS.  
- **Back‑end**: Node 20, Fastify (or Hapi) + TypeScript, PostgreSQL 16 (pg-promise), Redis caching.  
- **Auth**: OAuth 2.0 with Auth0, JWT, secure session store.  
- **Testing**: Vitest for unit, Playwright for end‑to‑end.  
- **Container/CI**: Docker, Docker Compose, GitLab CI pipelines, Helm for k8s deployments.  
- **Misc**: GraphQL optional (Apollo Client 4.x on front‑end, GraphQL‑Yoga on back‑end), but can fallback to REST. Need someone who can flex between GraphQL and REST.  

We’re building a consumer‑facing SaaS platform that has to run on AWS EKS, so AWS knowledge (ECR, RDS, SQS) is a plus. The role is **full‑time, hybrid** (SF), but remote work is fine. Must hit #️⃣ ready to onboard by mid‑June 2026.

Think you’re a fit? Drop a quick Slack DM or an email with past projects and repo links. Let’s build something epic! 🚀

Implemented Auth0 OAuth 2.0 authentication, JWT session handling, and deployed on AWS EKS using Helm charts. Built automated GitLab CI pipelines with Docker Compose, and leveraged AWS services such as ECR, RDS, and SQS for scalable operations.

anchor:
We’re looking for a **Frontend Engineer – Full‑Stack Web (React 18 / Next.js 13 / Tailwind 3.3)** to join our distributed team of product builders. The candidate should be able to dive into a codebase that uses **TypeScript 5.0**, **React 18** with concurrent rendering enabled, **Next.js 13** server‑side rendering via the new app/route‑based API, and Tailwind 3.3, while managing incremental migrations from a legacy CSS‑module system.

**Core responsibilities**

- Build reusable, high‑performance components with React 18 using the new hook `[useEffect, useSyncExternalStore]` plus dynamic, code‑splitting via Next.js dynamic imports.
- Convert an existing Storybook‑based design system into an accessible component library that follows WCAG 2.2 AA, automatically generating accessibility reports with **jest‑axe** and **axe-core**.
- Integrate the UI with **GraphQL** (Apollo Client 3.10) for data fetching, caching, and optimistic UI, including server‑side data hydration with Next.js `getServerSideProps`.
- Deliver end‑to‑end automation in **GitHub Actions**: lint (`eslint 8.45 + @typescript-eslint/parser`), format (`prettier 3.0`), unit tests (`jest 29`, `@testing-library/react 13`), and integration tests with **Playwright 1.40**.
- Maintain and improve the CI pipeline for PWA checks (service workers, manifest, Lighthouse >90) and performance regression tests.
- Collaborate with the DevOps team to ensure production readiness (Sourcemaps, Sentry error monitoring, Vercel deployments).

**Candidate profile**

- 2‑4 years of production experience building React 18‑based applications; experience with Next.js 13 app‑router and Edge functions is a must.
- Strong TypeScript fundamentals: type‑safety in components, custom hooks, and advanced generics (e.g., `useQuery<T>`).
- Proven knowledge of Tailwind CSS 3.3 utilities, custom plugins, and JIT mode for rapid icon and variant development.
- Comfortable with **GraphQL/Apollo** or similar state‑management patterns (e.g., SWR, React‑Query as a fallback option).
- Familiar with **Storybook** for design‑centric component-driven development and version‑control of stories.
- Excellent test‑driven development (TDD) mindset: unit, integration, and E2E tests for critical UI flows.
- Detail‑oriented user‑experience advocate: ability to translate Figma/Miro mockups into pixel‑perfect components, considering color contrast, keyboard navigation, ARIA roles, and screen‑reader support.
- Good communication skills, ability to mentor junior teammates, and ownership of small product features end‑to‑end.

**Benefits & logistics**

- Remote‑first role, with optional 3‑day office visits in San Francisco or Los Angeles (if you’re local).
- Flexible hours, 100% health, dental, vision, and a generous equity pool across all seniority levels.
- 20 days PTO, 10 company holidays, and 1 “mental‑health day” per year.
- Support for continuous learning (paid conferences, courses, and Slack communities).

positive.summary: Frontend Engineer with 4.5 years of experience focused on React 18, Next.js 13, and Tailwind CSS 3.3, specializing in component libraries, accessibility, and CI automation.

work_experience.description: Built reusable UI components, participated in design system migration, wrote unit tests with Jest, used GraphQL requests, improved accessibility with axe-core.

Developed high-performance UI components in React 18, built Next.js 13 app-router pages, integrated Apollo Client for GraphQL, authored Tailwind CSS utilities, automated CI with GitHub Actions, and mentored junior developers.

projects.description: Component library built with React 18 and Tailwind CSS 3.3, featuring accessibility testing via jest-axe and Storybook integration.

anchor:
**Frontend Engineer – Performance & PWA Specialist**

• **Core stack** – React 18+, Next.js 13+ (App Router & Server Components), TypeScript 5.x, Tailwind CSS 3.3  
• **UI/UX tooling** – Develop reusable UI primitives with Radix UI or Atomic Design principles; CSS modules or styled‑components (no Emotion) for scoped styling; maintain design tokens & color palettes in a Storybook design system  
• **Progressive Web App focus** – Implement Service Workers, Workbox caching strategies, push notifications, and offline UX flows; deepen knowledge of the Cache API, IndexedDB, and background sync  
• **Data layer** – GraphQL via Apollo Client 3+ with cache-first and cache-and-network policies; REST integration via Axios and React Query for imperative fetching; expose typed API hooks that handle loading, errors, and pagination  
• **Routing & SSR** – Leverage Next.js App Router for dynamic routing, edge functions, and ISR; build pages that prerender, hydrate, and split code efficiently for large content sites  
• **Motion & accessibility** – Write motion‑rich interfaces using Framer Motion 10+; audit with axe-core, WCAG 2.1 AA, and Lighthouse focus on a11y and Performance budgets  
• **Testing** – Unit tests: Jest + React Testing Library; Integration tests covering Service Worker registration and fallback UI; End‑to‑end tests with Cypress that simulate offline mode and APICalls  
• **CI/CD & code quality** – GitHub Actions pipeline that runs linting (ESLint, Prettier), unit tests, Cypress runs, and static analysis (SonarCloud). Follow GitFlow branching and code review best practices; enforce signed commits and WIP tag conventions  
• **Performance metrics** – Continuously measure Core Web Vitals, request size, JS bundle splitting, and First Input Delay; optimize image delivery with WebP, AVIF, and Cloudinary transforms  
• **Team & culture** – 2–4 years of production experience (mid‑level) with a proven track record of mentoring junior developers; experience in a distributed setting with 9–12 pm Pacific overlap; excellent written & spoken English; proactive in knowledge sharing (Brown Bag talks, tutorial blogs)  
• **Business context** – Prior startup or SaaS experience preferred; competency in fast‑paced product launches and rollback strategies (feature flags, canary releases)  
• **Job terms** – Full‑time, permanent role; remote‑first based in US West (Pacific) or willing to shift to EST; must pass a security background check; canstart within 3–6 weeks (extensions only on mutual agreement)

anchor:
**Cybersecurity – Cloud Security Engineer (Mid‑Level)**  
*Full‑Time, 5‑7 Years Experience, US‑Based, Remote‑Friendly (LA/Hollywood Area Preferred)*  

We are recruiting a mid‑seniority Cloud Security Engineer to spearhead our cloud‑native security program across AWS, Azure, and GCP environments for a fast‑growing SaaS company. The candidate will architect secure cloud solutions, implement robust IAM, firewall, and encryption controls, and advance our automated threat detection pipeline.  

**Key Responsibilities**  
- Own design, deployment, and continuous improvement of secure cloud infrastructure (AWS CloudFormation, Terraform, Azure ARM, GCP Deployment Manager).  
- Lead the implementation of Zero‑Trust principles, including least‑privilege IAM, dynamic provisioning, and SSO via SAML/IdP connectors for > 10,000 enterprise users.  
- Orchestrate Security‑by‑Design reviews, threat modeling, and penetration testing of cloud workloads; collaborate with DevOps to harden CI/CD pipelines (GitHub Actions, CircleCI, Azure Pipelines).  
- Develop and maintain incident‑response playbooks for cloud‑centric threats; interface with SOC‑2, ISO 27001, and SOC 3 audit engagements.  
- Mentor junior engineers, conduct security architecture workshops, and present findings to executive stakeholders.

**Required Qualifications**  
- 5–7 years of hands‑on cloud security experience (AWS Certified Security Specialty, CCSP, or equivalent).  
- Deep knowledge of AWS Security Hub, GuardDuty, KMS, IAM, and logging solutions; familiarity with Azure Security Center and GCP Security Command Center expected.  
- Strong scripting skills (Python, Bash, PowerShell) for automation and tooling.  
- Proven ability to design and enforce IAM, MFA, and role‑based access controls in large‑scale, multi‑tenant SaaS environments.  
- Experience with cloud‑native SIEM (Splunk, Datadog), SOAR, and threat‑intel integrations.  
- Excellent communication, stakeholder‑management, and cross‑functional leadership skills.  

**Preferred**  
- Demonstrated success with ESG compliance (SOC 2, ISO 27001, HITRUST).  
- Previous experience in a regulated industry (healthcare, finance) and handling security certifications.  

**Location & Flexibility**  
- Candidates located in Greater Los Angeles or eligible for remote work across US time zones.  
- Occasional travel (up to 10 %) to regional data centers and partner events.  

**We Offer**  
- Competitive salary, performance bonuses, and comprehensive benefits.  
- Clear career progression to Cloud Security Lead or Enterprise Security Architect within 18 months.  
- Dynamic, high‑growth environment focused on secure innovation.  

If you meet the above criteria and are ready to drive our cloud security strategy forward, please submit your résumé and a brief cover letter highlighting your most relevant achievements.

anchor:
**Senior Frontend Architect – Vue 3, Nuxt 3, TypeScript, SCSS, Vite**  
- 8 + years of production experience building enterprise‑grade single‑page applications (SPAs) and server‑rendered sites with Vue 3 (≥ 3.3.x) and Nuxt 3 (≥ 3.0.x).  
- Deep expertise in TypeScript ≥ 4.9, with a strong password on type‑safe component composition, Composition API, and custom plumbing (e.g., Pinia ≥ 2.0).  
- Proficient in the Vite build tool (≥ v4.x) including plugin ecosystem, custom asset handling, and full opt‑in lazy‑loading strategies for chunks, images, and dynamic components.  
- Demonstrated ability to design and ship complex, reusable UI component libraries (CSS‑in‑JS/SCSS modules, <style scoped>) with well‑documented APIs, release‑management (semantic‑release + changelogs).  
- Proven track record configuring Nuxt 3 for both SSR and static‑site generation (SSG) with intelligent route payload‑splitting, ISR, and incremental build caching.  
- Extensive experience writing end‑to‑end tests with Cypress 9.x, unit tests with Vitest 1.x, and testing‑oriented linting (ESLint 8.x, Prettier 2.x, w/ custom rules for Vue).  
- Skilled in progressive‑web‑app (PWA) architecture, service workers (Workbox), and advanced performance tuning (Web Vitals KPI, Lighthouse).  
- Deep familiarity with accessibility (WCAG 2.2 AA) and SEO for Vue/Nuxt, including semantic markup, proper meta‑tag injection, and structured‑data schema.  
- Prior senior leadership roles: mentoring teams, conducting code reviews, and leading architecture design reviews; comfortable with pair‑programming and critiquing in an agile sprint cadence.  
- Fluent in English communication; Spanish or French a plus for cross‑regional collaboration.

- Must hold an active AWS Certified Solutions Architect – Associate or equivalent; knowledge of Terraform or CloudFormation is a bonus.  
- Strong will to mentor junior developers and cultivate an engineering culture that prioritizes review, documentation, and quality.  
- Must be available for on‑site in San Francisco or Denver (within 30 min commute) or fully remote.

anchor:
**Junior Frontend Engineer – React 18, Next.js 13.4, Tailwind 3.3 (Full‑Time, Remote, US‑Tier 1)**  

We are looking for a **Junior Frontend Engineer** to join our digital products team, take ownership of improving UI quality, and increasingly collaborate with senior engineers on broader system design. This role will use the latest tech stack (~React 18, Next 13.4, Tailwind 3.3) and integrate modern tooling such as Vite, Vitest, and TanStack Query.

**Key Responsibilities**
- Implement and refine reusable UI components in React 18, building upon our shared component library while adhering to the company’s design system guidelines (Tailwind 3.3 + custom utility plugins).
- Contribute to the Next 13.4 “app” router architecture, writing API routes with edge functions and incrementally adopting streaming SSR where appropriate.
- Write and maintain comprehensive unit and integration tests using Vitest + Testing Library; add coverage thresholds (minimum 80 %).
- Collaborate with the Accessibility Champion to make components WCAG‑3‑ready, implementing ARIA attributes, focus management, and keyboard navigation support.
- Participate in daily stand‑ups, sprint planning, and code reviews, presenting proposals for improving maintainability and performance (e.g., lazy‑loading techniques, memoization patterns, bundle size checks with Webpack bundle analyzer).
- Work with the DevOps team to fine‑tune our Vite+ESLint+Prettier pipeline, ensuring CI/CD builds produce production‑ready artifacts.

**Desired Skill Set**
- **1–2 years of professional experience** building React web applications (preferred portfolio of open‑source or commercial projects).
- Proficiency in **React 18** (hooks, context, concurrent mode) and **TypeScript 5.0** (strong typing, interface design).
- Solid understanding of **Next.js 13.4** (app router, server components, middleware) and Tailwind 3.3 (configuring JIT, custom theme extensions).
- Familiarity with **motion libraries** (Framer Motion or React Spring) and **state‑management** patterns (React Query, TanStack Query, Zustand).
- Experience writing unit tests with **Vitest** or Jest, and component tests with **Testing Library**.
- Optional: background in **SVG animation**, **CSS Grid**, or **Web Components**.
- Comfortable using **GitHub Actions** for CI, Docker for local dev, and cloud environments (AWS, Azure, or GCP).
- Demonstrated growth mindset: active learner, proactive about staying up‑to‑date with React ecosystem, open-source contributions.

**Growth & Mentorship**
- Work under a senior engineer who will mentor you in advanced performance optimizations, server‑side rendering nuances, and component‑library governance.
- Opportunity to lead one project component (e.g., the “Header Nav” or “Auth Flow”) by project end of 6‑month sprint.

**Compensation & Perks**
- Competitive salary ($70k – 85k base) commensurate with experience, with potential for a $5k–$8k performance bonus annually.
- 100 % remote work flexibility, with optional in‑office days in the San Francisco / Seattle metro areas (paid travel stipend).
- $1k monthly tech‑stipend, quarterly team socials, and annual hackathon days.
- Full‑benefits package (health, dental, vision, 401(k), PTO) from Day 1.

**How to Apply**
Send your résumé, a brief cover letter explaining why you’re excited about this role, and links to at least two publicly available projects (GitHub, CodeSandbox, or deployed app) to hr@ourcompany.com with the subject line **“Junior Frontend Engineer – React/Next/Tailwind”**.

- Collaborate with the Accessibility Champion to make components WCAG‑3‑ready, implementing ARIA attributes, focus management, and keyboard navigation support.
- Participate in daily stand‑ups, sprint planning, and code reviews, presenting proposals for improving maintainability and performance (e.g., lazy‑loading techniques, memoization patterns, bundle size checks with Webpack bundle analyzer).
- Work with the DevOps team to fine‑tune our Vite+ESLint+Prettier pipeline, ensuring CI/CD builds produce production‑ready artifacts.

**Desired Skill Set**
- **1–2 years of professional experience** building React web applications (preferred portfolio of open‑source or commercial projects).
- Proficiency in **React 18** (hooks, context, concurrent mode) and **TypeScript 5.0** (strong typing, interface design).
- Solid understanding of **Next.js 13.4** (app router, server components, middleware) and Tailwind 3.3 (configuring JIT, custom theme extensions).
- Familiarity with **motion libraries** (Framer Motion or React Spring) and **state‑management** patterns (React Query, TanStack Query, Zustand).
- Experience writing unit tests with **Vitest** or Jest, and component tests with **Testing Library**.
- Optional: background in **SVG animation**, **CSS Grid**, or **Web Components**.
- Comfortable using **GitHub Actions** for CI, Docker for local dev, and cloud environments (AWS, Azure, or GCP).
- Demonstrated growth mindset: active learner, proactive about staying up‑to‑date with React ecosystem, open-source contributions.

**Growth & Mentorship**
- Work under a senior engineer who will mentor you in advanced performance optimizations, server‑side rendering nuances, and component‑library governance.
- Opportunity to lead one project component (e.g., the “Header Nav” or “Auth Flow”) by project end of 6‑month sprint.

**Compensation & Perks**
- Competitive salary ($70k – 85k base) commensurate with experience, with potential for a $5k–$8k performance bonus annually.
- 100 % remote work flexibility, with optional in‑office days in the San Francisco / Seattle metro areas (paid travel stipend).
- $1k monthly tech‑stipend, quarterly team socials, and annual hackathon days.
- Full‑benefits package (health, dental, vision, 401(k), PTO) from Day 1.

**How to Apply**
Send your résumé, a brief cover letter explaining why you’re excited about this role, and links to at least two publicly available projects (GitHub, CodeSandbox, or deployed app) to hr@ourcompany.com with the subject line **“Junior Frontend Engineer – React/Next/Tailwind”**.

anchor:
**Mid‑Level Frontend Engineer (Full‑Time, Remote – Chicago)** –  
We’re looking for a developer who can take ownership of a fast‑moving design‑system project built on Vue 3, Nuxt 3 and Tailwind 3.  

**Core Stack (versions)**  
- Vue 3.4.5 + Vue Router 4.2.3  
- Nuxt 3.6.7 (app‑dir, server‑components enabled)  
- Tailwind CSS 3.2.7 with JIT + core plugins (forms, aspect‑ratio)  
- Typescript 5.0.4 (strict mode, noImplicitAny)  
- Vite 4.0.2 (rollup 3, HMR, build perf)  
- Pinia 2.0.22 (state management)  
- GraphQL urql 4.0.9 (SSR + SWR via @urql/vue)  
- CSS‑in‑JS via Stitches 6.4.2 (utility‑first, theming)  

**Tooling & Ecosystem**  
- Unit tests: Vitest 0.32 + @testing-library/vue 8.0 (coverage ≥ 90 %)  
- End‑to‑end: Cypress 12.5 (cross‑browser, a11y plugin)  
- Accessibility: axe-core 4.4 via custom Cypress task; LCP, FCP & CLS measured with Google Lighthouse.  
- Dev‑Ops: Docker 24 (dev‑container), GitHub Actions (CI‑CD: lint → test → build → deploy to Netlify with preview branches)  
- Commit hygiene: husky‑v8 + lint‑staged (ESLint 8.56, Prettier 3.0) + commitlint 19.0.3 (conventional‑commits).  
- Storybook 7.0 (controls, docs, <font>StoryStore</font>) with Chromatic for visual regression; integration with Figma design tokens (via @figma-to-react).  

**Deliverables**  
1. Build a cookie‑free, PWA‑ready Nuxt site that uses Module Federation to load micro‑frontend widgets on demand.  
2. Create a fully‑typed component library in Stitches, with Storybook stories, a design‑token registry, and a shared lint‑and‑format config exported for consumption by the back‑end teams.  
3. Expose a public GraphQL API client that supports SSR over Nuxt and incremental static regeneration; cache responses in Pinia + IndexedDB for offline support.  
4. Integrate OAuth2 via Auth0, managing token refresh with a background SW (service‑worker) and ISO‑8601 token rotation.  
5. Ensure 100 % test coverage, a 95 % code‑coverage target for integration tests, and a CI step that flags failing audits (html‑validate, a11y‑check).  

**Experience & Skills**  
- 2–4 years professional experience in Vue/Nuxt development.  
- Deep knowledge of SSR, edge‑rendering and the Nuxt 3 SSR pipeline.  
- Proven track record designing modular, reusable component libraries that scale across a large codebase.  
- Comfortable with GitOps, can maintain public repo workflows and use GitHub PR templates to enforce linting, tests, and design‑token updates.  
- Familiar with security best‑practices (CORS, CSP, CSRF token management) and can write secure authentication flows.  
- Passion for open‑source: contributions to Vue, Tailwind, or Stitches repositories are a plus.  

**Hiring Detail**  
- Remote‑first, but must be available for bi‑weekly video syncs with the Chicago office.  
- Accustomed to an agile workflow (Scrum, Kanban) with sprint reviews, retrospectives, and a daily standup.  
- Must demonstrate ownership of a prior full‑stack project that shipped to production and can discuss performance optimization (bundle splitting, critical CSS).  

If you fit this profile and want to build a cutting‑edge Vue/Nuxt stack with a strong emphasis on performance, accessibility, and a seamless developer experience, we would love to learn more about you.

Dear Recruiting Team,

We are actively seeking a **Staff Machine Learning Engineer** with a focus on **TensorFlow, Hugging Face Transformers, and Apache Spark** for our AI Platform division. Candidates should possess **8+ years** of experience architecting and deploying scalable LLM solutions in multi‑cloud environments, demonstrating a strong record of moving projects from prototype to production at enterprise scale.

Key qualifications:
- Proven expertise in TensorFlow and Hugging Face libraries, with a comprehensive understanding of model training pipelines and optimization techniques.
- Deep proficiency in distributed data processing using Apache Spark, coupled with strong SQL skills for data extraction and feature engineering.
- Demonstrated ability to lead cross‑functional teams, mentor junior engineers, and drive best‑practice implementations across the AI lifecycle.
- Fluent in English (written and verbal); superior communication skills are essential for stakeholder engagement.
- Candidates must be comfortable with **remote work** but will be required to **travel to our Boston headquarters 3–4 times per quarter** to facilitate collaboration with the broader data science organization.

Employment terms:
- Full‑time, **Employee** status preferred; Contractor (C2C) may be considered with a preference for direct hire.
- Immediate availability to commence **within 4–6 weeks** of offer acceptance.

If you identify individuals who closely match this profile, kindly forward their resumes at your earliest convenience. We are eager to accelerate the hiring process and secure top talent to drive our next generation of AI capabilities.

Best regards,

[Your Title]  
[Company]

anchor: Seeking a motivated Junior Full‑Stack Web3 Developer (Rust & Solana) for a hybrid‑role, part‑time contract. Must possess 1‑3 years of experience building on Solana, writing on‑chain programs in Rust 1.70, using Anchor 0.26.x and Next.js 13 for the front‑end. Minimum requirements: package an Anchor program with the Solana CLI v1.14, deploy to devnet, test using mocha‑solana and webdriver.io, integrate wallet adapters (Phantom, Solflare, MathWallet) with @solana/wallet-adapter-react, and consume the Solana JSON‑RPC API via @solana/web3.js v1.73. Must demonstrate proficiency with Solana Program Library (SPL) token, escrow logic, and handling Serum DEX interactions. Experience writing unit tests in Rust with the anchor‑test framework (or cargo test + assert‑eq), bundle and build with wasm-pack or cargo‑build, and profiling with Solana Explorer metrics is a plus. The candidate should be familiar with deploying to devnet/testnet using the Solana cli and monitoring via the Solana wallet and explorer. Must be comfortable with version control via Git and automated CI using GitHub Actions, with deployments to Netlify or Vercel. Remote work is acceptable, but the role requires 5 days a week of synchronous overlap with the U.S. time‑zone. No prior cybersecurity clearance is required, but a basic understanding of on‑chain security best‑practices (RPC restrictions, BPF program safety) is mandatory. Candidates must be able to sign a standard NDA, commit to an agile sprint cadence (Scrum or Kanban), and should have contributed to at least one open‑source Solana program or front‑end dApp repository. Preferred are those with a background in Rust systems programming, a basic grasp of BPF bytecode optimization, and familiarity with Solana Anchor 0.28 tooling or newer. Immediate availability preferred.

anchor: Seeking a motivated Junior Full‑Stack Web3 Developer (Rust & Solana) for a hybrid‑role, part‑time contract. Must possess 1‑3 years of experience building on Solana, writing on‑chain programs in Rust 1.70, using Anchor 0.26.x and Next.js 13 for the front‑end. Minimum requirements: package an Anchor program with the Solana CLI v1.14, deploy to devnet, test using mocha‑solana and webdriver.io, integrate wallet adapters (Phantom, Solflare, MathWallet) with @solana/wallet-adapter-react, and consume the Solana JSON‑RPC API via @solana/web3.js v1.73. Must demonstrate proficiency with Solana Program Library (SPL) token, escrow logic, and handling Serum DEX interactions. Experience writing unit tests in Rust with the anchor‑test framework (or cargo test + assert‑eq), bundle and build with wasm-pack or cargo‑build, and profiling with Solana Explorer metrics is a plus. The candidate should be familiar with deploying to devnet/testnet using the Solana cli and monitoring via the Solana wallet and explorer. Must be comfortable with version control via Git and automated CI using GitHub Actions, with deployments to Netlify or Vercel. Remote work is acceptable, but the role requires 5 days a week of synchronous overlap with the U.S. time‑zone. No prior cybersecurity clearance is required, but a basic understanding of on‑chain security best‑practices (RPC restrictions, BPF program safety) is mandatory. Candidates must be able to sign a standard NDA, commit to an agile sprint cadence (Scrum or Kanban), and should have contributed to at least one open‑source Solana program or front‑end dApp repository. Preferred are those with a background in Rust systems programming, a basic grasp of BPF bytecode optimization, and familiarity with Solana Anchor 0.28 tooling or newer. Immediate availability preferred.

Managed CI pipelines via GitHub Actions and deployed the UI to Vercel.

Contributed to the open‑source Solana program 'EscrowLib' on GitHub, implementing SPL token escrow logic, optimizing BPF bytecode, and integrating Serum DEX interactions. Maintained automated CI with GitHub Actions, ensured compliance with Solana JSON‑RPC v1.73, and delivered front‑end updates on Next.js deployed via Vercel.

anchor:
We are seeking a **Frontend Architect – React/Next.js/Tailwind** to join our global product innovation team. The ideal candidate will bring *7+ years of senior‑level experience* designing and delivering large‑scale, highly‑available web applications that serve millions of users worldwide.  

Key responsibilities include:  
- Defining the end‑to‑end front‑end architecture, ensuring performance, scalability, and accessibility across all devices.  
- Leading a small team of engineers to implement best practices in component design, state management, and CI/CD pipelines.  
- Collaborating with product managers, UI/UX designers, and back‑end teams to translate complex requirements into elegant, production‑ready solutions using **React**, **Next.js** (Server‑Side Rendering + Incremental Static Regeneration), and **TailwindCSS**.  
- Driving code‑quality standards, conducting code reviews, and mentoring junior developers.  

Requirements:  
- Minimum **7 years** of hands‑on experience building high‑traffic, enterprise‑grade React applications.  
- Deep knowledge of **Next.js routing, ISR, SSR optimizations, and API data fetching patterns**.  
- Extensive experience with **TailwindCSS**, including customization, theming, and responsive design.  
- Proficiency with **TypeScript**, **GraphQL**, and modern front‑end build tooling (Webpack, Vite).  
- Strong expertise in **performance tuning** (Lighthouse, Web Vitals), **accessibility (WCAG 2.1)**, and **security best practices**.  
- Excellent communication skills and a proven track record of scaling engineering teams and processes.  

We offer a fully remote engagement, a competitive compensation package, and the opportunity to shape the future of our front‑end strategy.  

If you are a visionary leader ready to drive innovation at the intersection of design, performance, and scalability, we want to hear from you—available to start within 3–4 weeks.

anchor:
Hey team 👋 I’m on the hunt for a **Mid‑Level Platform & Infra Engineer** who can jump into our data lake world.  
Build pipelines in **Kafka + Airflow** + **Spark 3.x** so you’ll need 3‑5 yrs of hands‑on work with Kafka brokers, stream processing, and orchestration in Airflow.  
No sweat if you’ve used **dbt core** or dbt Cloud – just make sure you’re fluent in Python, SQL and can write Ansible/Terraform scripts to spin up infra.  
We’re all about automation: Docker, K8s, Jenkins or GitHub Actions.  

We’re a fast‑moving SaaS in the crypto space (but you can use any domain) and look for someone who can not only maintain pipelines but also improve reliability & performance.  
Full‑time, US‑based remote, no C2C. If you’re ready to dive into a stack that’s a mix of Kafka, Spark, and Airflow and help us hit our uptime SLA, hit me up! 🚀

anchor:
Recruiter,  
We are actively searching for a **Senior Full‑Stack Architect – MERN & GraphQL Lead** to spearhead the evolution of our enterprise web platform. This strategic role demands a seasoned engineer with **8–10 years of progressive experience** in the MERN ecosystem (MongoDB, Express, React, Node.js) coupled with demonstrable mastery of **GraphQL** (Apollo Federation, schema stitching, query optimization).  

Key responsibilities include:  
- Architecting and implementing a micro‑service‑oriented, scalable back‑end leveraging GraphQL services and RESTful APIs  
- Leading API gateway design, versioning strategies, and enforcing security best practices (JWT, OAuth2, CORS) across the stack  
- Championing containerization (Docker, Kubernetes) and establishing robust CI/CD pipelines (GitHub Actions, CircleCI) to ensure rapid, reliable deployments  
- Mentoring a high‑performing engineering squad, driving code quality, and facilitating agile ceremonies (Scrum / Kanban)  
- Collaborating cross‑functionally with Product Management, UX, and QA to translate business requirements into architecture roadmaps  

Essential qualifications:  
- Proven track record of delivering production‑grade MERN applications with GraphQL integrations in a large‑scale environment  
- Deep understanding of GraphQL server/client patterns, performance tuning, and federation concepts  
- Hands‑on expertise with TypeScript, React hooks, Next.js, and modern front‑end tooling (Webpack, Babel, ESLint)  
- Strong command over relational (PostgreSQL) and non‑relational (MongoDB) data modeling for GraphQL resolvers  
- Experience guiding teams on best practices in DevOps, observability, and fault‑tolerant design  
- Effective communication abilities to articulate complex technical concepts to executives and stakeholders  

We offer a **full‑time** engagement, flexible remote options, or hybrid setup in the New York City metro area. Please submit signed, NDA‑protected resumes; C2C is not accepted.  

If you are a forward‑thinking architect ready to shape the future of our digital experience, we’d love to connect.

work_experience.description: Architected a micro‑service oriented enterprise platform using MERN stack and Apollo Federation; led the transition to GraphQL APIs, designed API gateway with JWT/OAuth2, and implemented CI/CD pipelines with GitHub Actions and CircleCI.

Lead a squad of 12 engineers delivering a hospital‑appointment platform; championed containerization, observability with Prometheus & Grafana, and automated deployment pipelines; mentored developers on best practices and conducted agile ceremonies.

anchor:
We’re building a new SaaS analytics dashboard for mid‑market B2B and need a mid‑level Frontend Engineer to architect the UI layer, extend our UI component library, and ship performance‑critical features. The role focuses on:

* **React 18 (with concurrent mode enabled) + Next.js 13 (with the new app router and server components) + Tailwind CSS + Framer Motion**  
* **TypeScript 5.0** – strict type-checking, no “any” usage, and full support for React Conformance standards  
* **Component library** – create and publish reusable, test‑first components (Storybook 7, Vitest, E2E with Playwright) for marketing, admin, and partner portals  
* **Performance & accessibility** – implement route‑level code‑splitting, dynamic image optimization, and WCAG 2.2 level AA compliance; enforce core‑web‑vitals target of < 1 GB page weight and 90 + score on Lighthouse  
* **Stakeholder collaboration** – iterate on design system (Figma integration via *figma-to-typescript*), work alongside the product team to refine user journeys, and lead bi‑weekly demo sessions  

**Requirements**  

1. 2–4 years of production experience building complex React applications for SaaS or e‑commerce, with a portfolio of GitHub or live projects  
2. Deep knowledge of Next.js 13 features: server components, middleware, dynamic routing, image and font optimization  
3. Proven experience in building/deploying a critical component library (Storybook + automated tests) that scales across multiple teams  
4. Fluent in Tailwind CSS, Headless UI, and encouraging a “no‑CSS‑mix‑and‑match” culture; comfortable working with the @apply directive and arbitrary variants  
5. Experience with motion libraries (Framer Motion 9 or React Spring) and familiar with the new CSS “motion” support for reduced motion detection  
6. Comfortable writing Style‑First tests (Vitest + jest‑testing‑library), linting with ESLint + TypeScript plugin, and automating builds with Vite for lib targets  
7. Familiar with auto‑generation of design tokens (Style Dictionary) and integration into your component library  

**What we offer**  

* Full‑time, remote‑first role (US‑East, US‑West, or applicable EU locations) with a hybrid option on a short list.  
* Competitive base salary + quarterly equity, comprehensive health, and flexible PTO.  
* Hands‑on mentorship from our Senior Engineers, fast‑track promotion path, and the chance to lead our open‑source component library in the next 12 months.  

If you thrive at the intersection of cutting‑edge technology, performance, and design systems, we’d love to meet. Send us your portfolio and a brief message on why you’re the perfect fit for this role.

positive.summary: Mid-level Frontend Engineer with 3 years of experience building high-performance SaaS dashboards and reusable component libraries using React 18, Next.js 13, and Tailwind CSS.

work_experience.description: Developed marketing and admin portals for Careem’s B2B SaaS suite. Implemented Next.js 13 server components, dynamic routing, and image optimization. Built a component library with Storybook 7, Vitest, and Playwright, enforcing strict TypeScript typing and accessibility. Leveraged Tailwind CSS and Headless UI to maintain a no‑CSS‑mix‑and‑match culture.

Architected the UI layer for Swvl’s new analytics dashboard targeting mid‑market B2B clients. Extended the internal component library to support marketing, admin, and partner portals. Achieved core‑web‑vitals < 1 GB and Lighthouse scores above 90 through route‑level code‑splitting, dynamic image handling, and WCAG 2.2 AA compliance. Lead bi‑weekly demo sessions with product and design teams, integrating Figma‑to‑Typescript for design tokens.

Hey AI enthusiasts! We’re building a real‑time content moderation engine at BlockChainGuard and need a **ML Ops Engineer (mid‑seniority, 3‑5 yrs experience)** who can seamlessly deploy and scale high‑performance inference models. Your day‑to‑day will involve:

* **TensorFlow 2.9** (or later) model packaging and optimization with TensorRT and TF‑Serve  
* Building reproducible **Docker** containers and deploying them to **Kubernetes 1.29** clusters  
* Writing **Terraform** scripts for provisioning GPU‑enabled nodes on GCP (or Cloud‑flare Workers)  
* Monitoring model health with **Prometheus / Grafana** and auto‑scaling via **KEDA**  
* Setting up A/B testing pipelines and blue‑green deployments for A/B tested recommender models  
* Collaborating with our Data Scientists to perform **model pruning** and **quantization** and ensuring 1‑second latency targets  

We value a hacker mindset, strong TL;DR reporting skills, and a curiosity for cleaner, greener AI infrastructure. Remote‑friendly, full‑time, starts June‑15. Drop me a line if you’re ready to get your hands dirty with cutting‑edge ML production at the edge of blockchain!
