anchor:
Frontend Engineer – React 17 + Next.js 12 – Tailwind 2.2 (Junior / Mid‑Level)

**Experience & Skills**  
- 1–2 years of production‐grade web development in a SaaS or consumer product.  
- Proficient in **React 17** (Hooks, Concurrent Mode, StrictMode) and **Next.js 12** (ISR, API Routes, file‑system routing, optional server‑less functions).  
- Deep familiarity with **Tailwind 2.2** utilities, responsive design, and custom `@apply` rules.  
- Strong TypeScript 4.8 fundamentals; no requirement for pure TS mastery.  
- Comfortable using CSS Modules or SCSS alongside Tailwind; can roll your own PostCSS plugins.  
- Preferred: experience building a **component library** in **Storybook 6.x** (Chromatic integration), and writing unit tests with **Jest + Enzyme**.  
- Acceptance of **E2E** testing in **Cypress 10**; ability to author automated visual regression checks.  
- Standard build tooling with **Vite 4** (or legacy Webpack 5 if project demands).  
- Hooks into **GitLab CI/CD** pipelines that run type‑checking, linting, unit tests, and deploy previews to **Netlify** on every merge request.  
- Demonstrated use of **Redux Toolkit** or **Zustand** for local state.  
- Solid understanding of **W3C accessibility** standards (WCAG 2.1 AA) for component authoring.  
- Enthusiastic about performance: importing only needed font subsets, lazy‑loading images, and ensuring bundle size ≤ 500 KB per page.  

**Responsibilities**  
- Write clean, maintainable React components that adhere to the brand’s design system.  
- Contribute to the internal dev‑ops pipeline: updating npm dependencies, maintaining Dockerfiles (optional), and ensuring safe releases.  
- Mentor interns and junior front‑end contributors; provide constructive code reviews.  
- Participate in sprint planning (2‑week cycles), estimate effort, and manage time‑boxes responsibly.  
- Deliver rigorous documentation: README, Storybook stories, and inline JSDoc comments.  

**Nice to Have**  
- Exposure to **SWR** for data fetching; or familiar with **Axios** and **React Query**.  
- Basic knowledge of **i18next** or `next-i18next` for localization.  
- Experience with **Keycloak** or `next-auth` for authentication (although not required).  

**Work Arrangement**  
- Remote‑friendly, but must overlap with US Eastern Time for regular stand‑ups.  
- Hybrid contract (3‑month pilot) or full‑time (L5) positions open.  
- Compensation: $85k–$108k (depending on location) or equivalent Contractor Rate.  
- Start Flex: available any Q4 2026.  

**How to Apply**  
Submit a portfolio or GitHub link with at least two finished projects that demonstrate your use of the above stack, along with a concise resume. Writers who can show a Storybook harness or a production build that loads < 500 KB will receive head‑count priority.

anchor:
**Front‑End Web3 Engineer (React/Next.js 13) – Mid-Level**

- **Core Responsibilities**  
  - Build responsive, accessible dApp UIs using React 18 (Functional Components, Hooks, Suspense) and Next.js 13 (App Router).  
  - Integrate Ethereum wallets (MetaMask, WalletConnect, Coinbase Wallet) via `web3modal@3.x` and `ethers.js@5.7`.  
  - Develop token allowance flows, NFT galleries, and governance dashboards with TypeScript 5, Chakra UI 2.x, and TailwindCSS 3.x.  
  - Implement server‑side rendering (SSR) for SEO‑critical pages and pre‑fetch transaction data using Next.js API routes and `cached` ethers provider.  
  - Write unit and e2e tests with Jest 29, React Testing Library 13, and Cypress 12, targeting 80%+ coverage.  
  - Create custom hooks for wallet detection, chain switching, and real‑time gas fee estimation (using `@ethersproject/providers`).  

- **Tech Stack Requirements**  
  - **Languages & Frameworks**: TypeScript 5, React 18, Next.js 13 (App Router), Chakra UI 2.x, TailwindCSS 3.x.  
  - **Blockchain Libraries**: ethers.js 5.7, web3modal @3.x, @walletconnect/client 2.x.  
  - **Testing & CI**: Jest 29, React Testing Library 13, Cypress 12, GitHub Actions (Node 20 runner).  
  - **Build Tools**: Vite 4.x for component bundles, Prettier 3.x, ESLint 8.x with `airbnb-typescript` configuration.  
  - **Version Control**: Git, GitFlow with protected branches and Pull‑Request reviews.  

- **Experience & Skills**  
  - 2–4 years of professional front‑end development with a portfolio of live Web3 projects or dApp prototypes.  
  - Proven track record on at least one dApp deployed on Ethereum mainnet or a major L2 (Optimism, Arbitrum, zkSync).  
  - Familiarity with UI/UX best practices for blockchain interfaces: clipboard copy, transaction status badges, gas cost notifications.  
  - Experience with OAuth2 or OpenID Connect integration for off‑chain user auth (e.g., NextAuth v4).  
  - Comfortable working in Agile/Scrum teams with Jira, maintaining CI/CD pipelines and deploying to Vercel or Netlify.  

- **Desirable Extras**  
  - Knowledge of NFT metadata standards (ERC‑1155, ERC‑721), IPFS interactions, and ALCHEMY or INFURA APIs.  
  - Comfortable with design tokens, theming in Chakra UI, and responsive design for mobile wallet usage.  
  - Spanish or French speaking ability is a plus for regional product launches.  

- **Logistics**  
  - Full‑time, permanent role (no C2C).  
  - Base location: Remote (U.S. Central or equivalent); will cooperate with U.S. time zones (10:00–19:00 CEST).  
  - Start within 2–4 weeks; must sign a standard NDA.  

**Application**  
Submit a résumé, links to GitHub repositories or hosted dApps, and a brief cover letter explaining your experience building a Web3 front‑end solution.
